novel_features.name: Full offline AI-referenceable clone
novel_features.description: Pull a municipality's entire code into a local store and an AI-ready Markdown/text tree in one command, so an agent can reference it offline with no further API calls.
novel_features.rationale: Combines a full TOC+content sync, FTS indexing, and an ordinance-history lineage index into one local artifact that no single API call or existing tool produces.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first when an agent needs to read or search a whole city's code repeatedly without hitting the live API each time.

novel_features.name: Clone-vs-live drift diff
novel_features.description: See which sections of a city's code changed in the live code since you cloned it: added, removed, or reworded.
novel_features.rationale: Compares your local clone against the current upstream code to surface section-level drift that no single API call reports.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to detect exactly what changed in a code since you last cloned it.

novel_features.name: Cross-city topic comparison
novel_features.description: Lay one topic (e.g. short-term rentals) side by side across several cities, aligning each city's controlling section.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a full-text join across documents from multiple synced municipalities that exists only in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to compare how different jurisdictions regulate the same activity in one shot.

novel_features.name: Ordinance-history extraction
novel_features.description: Turn a section's '(Ord. No. ..., date)' history annotations into structured ordinance / section / date rows.
novel_features.rationale: Parses a municipal-code-specific content pattern out of stored section text; nothing upstream exposes it as data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to cite the enacting ordinances for a section, or invert with --by-ordinance to see everything an ordinance changed.

novel_features.name: Intra-code cross-reference graph
novel_features.description: List what a section cites and what cites it, building an inbound/outbound reference graph for a city's code.
novel_features.rationale: Requires extracting citation references from stored text and joining them to the TOC tree in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to trace how sections of a code reference each other before relying on a single section in isolation.

novel_features.name: Defined-term lookup
novel_features.description: Return the one authoritative definition of a term from a code's Definitions sections, with its citation.
novel_features.rationale: Scopes FTS to Definitions-family headings in the local store and extracts the defining clause, not a hit list.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need the controlling definition of a term, not general full-text hits.

novel_features.name: Mirror freshness check
novel_features.description: List synced codes whose upstream codification is newer than your local copy, so you know which mirrors to re-sync.
novel_features.rationale: Compares the local sync cursor against the upstream latest job per municipality — a cross-entity check, not one API call's output.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find out-of-date local mirrors before trusting or publishing their text.

narrative.headline: Every Municode feature, plus an offline local mirror, real full-text search, change tracking, and citation tooling no other Municode tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Browse and read 3,300+ US municipal codes from the command line, then clone any city's code into a local SQLite database for offline reading and FTS5 search that works even though Municode's own full-text search is a paid MuniPro feature. On top of that local store it adds clone-vs-live drift detection (diff), side-by-side cross-city topic comparison (compare), structured ordinance-history extraction (history), an intra-code cross-reference graph (xref), and authoritative defined-term lookup (defs).
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required — Municode's public API at api.municode.com serves municipal codes, ordinances, and table-of-contents data to anyone. Note: upstream full-text search is a paid 'MuniPro' tier and returns no results for free/anonymous users, which is exactly why this CLI builds its own local FTS index over cloned content.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI and api.municode.com are reachable before anything else.
Resolve a city name to its addressable code (clientId, productId, latest jobId).
Browse the code's table of contents to find the chapter you need.
Mirror the whole code into local SQLite so search and the novel commands work offline.
Full-text search the cloned code locally — works without the paid MuniPro tier.
Pull the authoritative definition of a term with its citation.

troubleshoots.fix: Run 'municode-pp-cli clone "<City, ST>"' first — search reads the local FTS index, not the paid upstream search.

troubleshoots.symptom: a local command says the city is not cloned
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'municode-pp-cli clone "<City, ST>"' to mirror that city's code before querying it offline.
Use the exact municipality name and two-letter state, e.g. "St. Petersburg, FL"; list candidates with 'municode-pp-cli cities --state FL'.
These read the local store — clone the city first. diff reports changes since you cloned, so a freshly cloned code shows no drift.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs to read, search, or analyze the actual text of US municipal codes and ordinances — zoning, building, licensing, definitions — across one or many cities. It is the right tool for offline legal-text research, citation building, comparing how different jurisdictions regulate a topic, and tracking how a code changed between codification supplements.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for federal or state statutes — it only covers municipal/county codes hosted on Municode.
Do not use it to obtain paid MuniPro full-text search results; the free tier has no upstream search index.
Do not use it as legal advice or an authoritative legal citation source without verifying against the official adopted code.
Do not use it for cities not hosted on library.municode.com (many use American Legal, eCode360, or Code Publishing instead).

recipes.title: Compare a regulation across cities (agent-friendly, narrowed)
recipes.explanation: After cloning both cities, aligns the controlling section per city and narrows the deeply nested response to just city, citation, and heading.

recipes.title: Read a section as clean text
recipes.explanation: Fetches a chapter/section chunk and returns each section's title and HTML-stripped plaintext.

recipes.title: Detect what changed since you cloned
recipes.explanation: Compares your local clone against the current live code and reports added, removed, and reworded sections with citations.

recipes.title: Find stale local mirrors
recipes.explanation: Lists every cloned city whose upstream codification is newer than your local copy so you know what to re-clone.

trigger_phrases: what does the city code say about short-term rentals
find the definition of dwelling unit in the municipal code
clone a municipal code for offline search
